texto: Sometimes it seems that men--yes, and even women too!--if they believe in a thing strongly enough, they get a strength or endurance that they wouldn't have believe possible.
explicacao: Citação de Carter Dickson sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Sometimes it seems that men--yes, and even women too!--if they believe in a thing strongly enough…” articula tempo, belief, endurance em registro de tempo, com relevância para contextos como termino e endurance.

texto: That sounds silly, doesn't it? Running away just because you don't want to hurt somebody's feelings? But did you ever think how much of our lives we spend,dodging and twisting and making things difficult for ourselves, to avoid hurting somebody's feelings. Even people with absolutely no claim on us?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Carter Dickson que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “That sounds silly, doesn't it? Running away just because you don't want to hurt somebody's feelin…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, feelings, somebody, com foco em termino e reflexao.

texto: Put it in terms of the not-too-serious, if you like. Who got into that locked room? And how was it done? And why should the cup have been moved again? We're up against the essential detective problems of who, how, and why. Simply because there was no murder or near-murder, does that make the mystery one bit less baffling?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Carter Dickson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Put it in terms of the not-too-serious, if you like. Who got into that locked room? And how was i…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, mystery, murder pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

We're up against the essential detective problems of who, how, and why. Simply because there was no murder or near-murder, does that make the mystery one bit less baffling?

texto: I never met a gal who represented a mystery to me in quite the fetchin' way you did. It'd be dull and dreary just to find out how a crook got in and out of a locked room to steal a gold-and-jewelled cup. But it's very rummy, and fascinates the old man a bit, to wonder why a crook didn't steal a gold-and-jewelled cup he should have stolen.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Carter Dickson sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “I never met a gal who represented a mystery to me in quite the fetchin' way you did. It'd be dull…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, mystery, crook, útil em momentos de reflexao.

texto: When a man can't sleep, he won't let anybody else sleep either. If he doesn't go off to dreamland the moment his head hits the pillow, he gets frightfully annoyed and won't stay in bed.
explicacao: Citação de Carter Dickson sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “When a man can't sleep, he won't let anybody else sleep either. If he doesn't go off to dreamland…” articula motivacao, sleep, reflexao em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.
